meaning: n.平底船,撑船,[足][橄]踢悬空球(球未落地前踢出)v.踢凌空球
examples: The investment is little more than a punt.
The referee told him to punt or kick the ball off the ground.
He caught a punt and scored the winning touchdown, with a minute left to play.
If Joe were still here, he would tell you: Sometimes it's smarter to punt.
The Giants had the ball on their own 26-yard line after a San Francisco punt.
